pi_items.source: O*NET / BLS SOC definitions matching the task prompt role.
Task description sets the role-play context as "You are a software engineer at a SaaS company."
BLS SOC system for major group 15-0000.
setup_task.sh scripts create bugs specifically requiring these skills (e.g., timedelta days vs hours, timezone boundary crossing, overlapping interval check, RFC 5545 format strings).
verifier.py calculates pass by checking if score >= 60, with each of 5 bugs worth 20 points.

privileged_info_summary: The task correctly operates on synthetically generated Python code representing a calendar scheduling engine. It correctly requires Software Developer skills (SOC 15-1252.00) in an Enterprise SaaS role-play context. The bugs involve datetime arithmetic, timezone handling during DST transitions, interval overlap logic, and RFC 5545 standard compliance. The verification logic correctly evaluates these fixes with a passing threshold of 60 points (requiring 3 out of 5 bugs to be fixed at 20 points each).
